what_it_does: Defines the HTML entry shell for the ConceptV Studio design system manager, links app stylesheets, creates the React mount node, and loads external React/Babel runtimes plus local registry, service, component, canvas, and App.jsx scripts.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment naming the app, subtitle, group, and 1440x800 viewport.
Loads six local stylesheets before the app markup and scripts.
Creates a single root mount element with id="app".
Loads React, ReactDOM, and Babel from unpkg with integrity and crossorigin attributes.
Uses local icon, registry, service, shared component, canvas, workshop canvas, and app entry scripts.
Mixes plain JavaScript script tags with type="text/babel" JSX script tags.
Groups script includes with descriptive HTML comments.
standouts: Acts as a manifest-like ordered loader for the app surface.
The script list exposes a broad studio scope: registry, imagery, AI, workshop, components, settings, and multiple canvases.
The page relies on browser-loaded Babel for JSX modules.
conventions: Uses relative local asset paths throughout the project tree.
Uses PascalCase filenames for React component and canvas JSX files.
Uses section comments to organize dependencies by role.
Loads foundational libraries before local modules, and App.jsx last.
Keeps the HTML body minimal, with app structure delegated to loaded scripts.
concerns: Runtime JSX compilation through Babel is visible in the shipped HTML.
The file has many ordered script dependencies, so load order is implicit and fragile from this file alone.
External CDN scripts are required for the page to initialize.
notes: This is the app bootstrap document that wires styles, runtimes, modules, and the React entry point together.
questions: Is this intended as a development-only handoff shell or a production entry document?
Are all script dependencies required on initial load, or could some canvases be loaded lazily?
What component mounts into #app from App.jsx?
purpose_vs_actual: The title and dsCard metadata describe a ConceptV Studio design system manager, and the loaded styles, registry, services, components, canvases, and App.jsx align with that purpose.
intention_signals: The file seems intended to assemble a browser-run React studio from modular local scripts while preserving readable handoff structure through comments and grouped includes.
novelty: The dsCard metadata comment and the large browser-side JSX module manifest are distinct within this file.
contribution: Provides a concrete app entry ledger: metadata, stylesheet set, runtime libraries, registry pipeline, service layer, reusable components, canvas inventory, and final application bootstrap order.
summary_for_embedding: HTML entry file for ConceptV Studio that links styles, creates the #app mount point, and loads React/Babel plus local registry, service, component, canvas, and App.jsx modules.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for bootstrapping a browser-based design system manager studio with registry, AI, imagery, workshop, and canvas modules available at startup.
